content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Memory v3 storage tiers separate prompt, typed memory, notes, evidence, and indexes; The Memory v3 architecture defines five storage tiers: Tier A Core / in-context memory for small prompt-bound blocks; Tier B Structured long-term memory for typed memory; Tier C Rich note memory for semantic notes and links; Tier D Raw evidence for immutable or nearly immutable source material; and Tier E Retrieval indexes for vector, full-text, and filter indexes even if physically stored in one Postgres.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope', storage tier design
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Memory v3 Read path retrieval modes, budgets, and answer composition; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the Memory v3 read path first decides whether memory is needed, loads core blocks, chooses Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, or Evidence-first/evidence-fallback retrieval, enforces explicit `v3` budget caps by query type, and composes answers that distinguish verified from provisional information, account for conflicts and scope boundaries, can abstain, and do not present notes as hard facts without qualification.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path', Read path steps 1-5
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended Memory v3 write and read architecture flow; The recommended architecture logs User / Tool / Document input in Source Log, routes it through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval, canonicalizes entities, dispatches to Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, reconciles claims and notes into Postgres + pgvector, runs background consolidation/repair/retention workers, and serves runtime answers through Read-Time Retrieval Planner and Answer Composer.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Memory v3 Write path operational sequence; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the Memory v3 write path persists raw source input, routes it to `noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, or `mixed`, retrieves candidate memories before extraction, canonicalizes entities, runs specialized extractors, reconciles candidates, applies all changes in one transaction, and schedules maintenance such as consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention/archive.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path', Write path steps 1-8
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Memory v3 responsibility split between runtime, worker, and storage; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, responsibility is split so the User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; the Memory worker / manager handles routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; and the Storage layer handles immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности'
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Optional Memory v3 fast/slow execution path; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the optional post-MVP fast/slow execution path separates a user-facing, lower-latency Fast path for cheap retrieval, cheap draft, and escalation gate from a Slow path using a stronger model/worker for conflict resolution, answer verification, note consolidation, and memory repair. Fast/slow coordination should pass **typed handoff objects** rather than free text.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting .. 16. Optional fast/slow execution path', Optional fast/slow execution path
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_semantic_write_path; The Memory v3 system has Note path as a semantic write path.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3-4
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> requires_write_task; The Memory write path requires applying entity upserts, claim operations, note operations, task operations, profile projection updates, and memory run log storage in one transaction.; scope=Applies within document agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11. Write path.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> primary_storage_backend; The recommended Memory v3 architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as its primary storage backend.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 4 recommended architecture
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must not turn every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> uses_handoff_format; The Memory v3 fast and slow paths should exchange typed handoff objects instead of free text.; scope=Applies to communication between Fast path and Slow path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store behavior; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, `Note` should have a maturity lifecycle so notes do not become a summary-store: `fresh`, `stabilizing`, `mature`, and `consolidated`. Maturity can increase through retrieval use, confirmation by multiple sources, lack of conflict, time without supersede, and importance for recurring rationale questions; it can decrease or close through explicit supersede, stale scope, lack of use, or weak evidence support.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair', Note maturity
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; Truth state and retrieval state must stay separate; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, `Truth state` answers whether an item is currently valid as knowledge, while `Retrieval state` answers how it should be searched and kept in the hot path. They must not be mixed: for example, a note can be `resolved` but still `warm` for rationale retrieval, and a claim can be `superseded` but still `warm` as historical evidence.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state'
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> performs_function; The Slow path in Memory v3 performs note consolidation.; scope=Applies to the Slow path described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md.
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; Memory repair is separate from consolidation; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, `Repair` is a separate background workflow that fixes duplicate notes or claims, contradictory active items, profile drift, stale task states, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage. It is distinct from consolidation: consolidation enriches memory, while repair keeps memory healthy.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair', Memory repair
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> requires_write_task; The Memory write path requires persisting raw input including text, source type, speaker, timestamp, namespace, thread/conversation IDs, and optional embedding.; scope=Applies within document agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11. Write path.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_architecture_axis; Memory v3 architecture has Scope and lineage as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Entity; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=Entity (memory architecture); reason=Mention refers to an internal memory-model concept, not an existing candidate.
- mention=Claim; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Claim (memory architecture); reason=Mention refers to an internal memory-model concept, not an existing candidate.
- mention=NoteLink; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=NoteLink; reason=Mention is a durable architecture term with no safe existing match.
- mention=scope_json; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=scope_json; reason=Mention is a durable schema/architecture term with no safe existing match.
- mention=Postgres; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=PostgreSQL; reason=Postgres is a common alias for PostgreSQL and no existing candidate matches.
- mention=JSONB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=JSONB; reason=Mention is a concrete database technology with no existing candidate.
- mention=pgvector; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=pgvector; reason=Mention is a concrete technology with no existing candidate.
- mention=full-text indexes; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<7>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Full-text indexes; reason=Mention is a reusable storage/search technology concept with no existing candidate.
- mention=lineage tables; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<8>; type=CONCEPT; name=Lineage tables; reason=Mention is a durable architecture/storage concept with no existing candidate.
- mention=Graph DB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<9>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Graph database; reason=Mention refers to a database technology and no existing candidate safely matches.
- mention=traversal/path queries; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<10>; type=CONCEPT; name=Traversal/path queries; reason=Mention is a reusable query concept relevant to graph databases.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<11>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=01. Architecture|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=01. Architecture agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB MARKDOWN Document title: 01. Architecture Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB Document lines: 547-558 ## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics. Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:9ab04f0af9d4842289e56dca233345ed] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; title=01. Architecture; heading=01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
